If unique identification is required, use departments.code (unique) instead; this plan uses name for readability.","Joins must use scholarships.student_id = students.id and students.department_id = departments.id as listed in filters."]},"attempt":2,"maxAttempts":4,"previousAttempt":{"sql":"WITH dept_total AS (
  SELECT
    sch.academic_year AS academic_year,
    d.name AS department_name,
    SUM(sch.amount) AS total_scholarship_amount
  FROM public.scholarships AS sch
  JOIN public.students AS stu ON sch.student_id = stu.id
  JOIN public.departments AS d ON stu.department_id = d.id
  WHERE sch.amount IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY sch.academic_year, d.name
)
SELECT
  academic_year,
  department_name,
  total_scholarship_amount
FROM (
  SELECT
    academic_year,
    department_name,
    total_scholarship_amount,
    RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY academic_year ORDER BY total_scholarship_amount DESC) AS dept_rank
  FROM dept_total
) AS ranked
WHERE dept_rank = 1
ORDER BY academic_year ASC, department_name ASC;","validationIssues":["academic_year is treated as ordinal/nominal (string) in the visualization — if academic_year is a true temporal value (or should be normalized), consider using a temporal axis or normalizing the field first.","If there are ties (RANK() used in SQL), the chart will render multiple bars for the same academic_year (grouped by color) — confirm this grouped-bar behavior matches the desired presentation (vs. e.g., a single label or stacked representation).","Metadata rowCount is 4 but the provided sample contains 2 rows — minor inconsistency in metadata.sample vs metadata.rowCount."]},"historySummary":"Attempt 1: passed","latest":{"sqlDraft":{"reasoning":"Aggregate scholarship amounts per department and academic year, use RANK() partitioned by academic_year to include ties, and return departments with rank = 1. passed"]}},"endedAt":1771846920886}},"input":{"question":"Which department received the most total scholarship money each academic year?"},"result":{"sql":"WITH dept_total AS (
  SELECT
    sch.academic_year AS academic_year,
    d.name AS department_name,
    SUM(sch.amount) AS total_scholarship_amount
  FROM public.scholarships AS sch
  JOIN public.students AS stu ON sch.student_id = stu.id
  JOIN public.departments AS d ON stu.department_id = d.id
  WHERE sch.amount IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY sch.academic_year, d.name
)
SELECT
  academic_year,
  department_name,
  total_scholarship_amount
FROM (
  SELECT
    academic_year,
    department_name,
    total_scholarship_amount,
    RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY academic_year ORDER BY total_scholarship_amount DESC) AS dept_rank
  FROM dept_total

visualization.title: Top scholarship-recipient department by academic year (total amount)

resultRaw.sql:
WITH dept_total AS (
  SELECT
    sch.academic_year AS academic_year,
    d.name AS department_name,
    SUM(sch.amount) AS total_scholarship_amount
  FROM public.scholarships AS sch
  JOIN public.students AS stu ON sch.student_id = stu.id
  JOIN public.departments AS d ON stu.department_id = d.id
  WHERE sch.amount IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY sch.academic_year, d.name
)
SELECT
  academic_year,
  department_name,
  total_scholarship_amount
FROM (
  SELECT
    academic_year,
    department_name,
    total_scholarship_amount,
    RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY academic_year ORDER BY total_scholarship_amount DESC) AS dept_rank
  FROM dept_total
) AS ranked
WHERE dept_rank = 1
ORDER BY academic_year ASC, department_name ASC;

issues: academic_year is treated as ordinal/nominal (string) in the visualization — if academic_year is a true temporal value (or should be normalized), consider using a temporal axis or normalizing the field first.

If there are ties (RANK() used in SQL), the chart will render multiple bars for the same academic_year (grouped by color) — confirm this grouped-bar behavior matches the desired presentation (vs. e.g., a single label or stacked representation).
Metadata rowCount is 4 but the provided sample contains 2 rows — minor inconsistency in metadata.sample vs metadata.rowCount.
